commit.message:
Fix login flow, tunnel status, admin rename, consolidate portal

- fix: login server action called wrong endpoint (GET→POST /api/portal/login)
- fix: isRedirectError re-throw to prevent catch block swallowing Next.js redirects
- fix: tunnel status stuck offline — add UpdateTunnelOnline() called on agent poll
- fix: NEXT_PUBLIC_CONTROL_API_URL use relative /control path to avoid mixed content
- feat: admin can inline-rename tunnel hostnames (pencil icon in tunnels drawer)
- feat: new API route /api/admin/routes/[id] for hostname rename with uniqueness check
- refactor: /portal login page replaced with redirect to /login (single entry point)
- fix: /portal/dashboard redirects to /login (not /portal), handles tunnel_id query param
  from server action redirect to initialize localStorage credentials

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: Skills now match workflows by category, tags, and name keywords

Root cause: analyzeWorkflow didn't pass workflow category/tags to
selectRelevantSkills, so custom skills with matching category (e.g.,
'compliance' skill for 'compliance-posture' workflow) were never selected.

Fixes:
- analyzeWorkflow accepts workflowMeta param (category, tags, workflowId)
- selectRelevantSkills now receives category + tags + workflow name keywords
- Workflow name is split into keywords for tag matching (e.g., 'HIPAA
  Compliance Check' → matches skill with tag 'HIPAA')
- API route passes workflowCategory, workflowTags, workflowId from client
- ExecutionPanel accepts workflowMeta, passes to analyze API call
- WorkflowsContent looks up workflow category/tags from catalog list

Example: HIPAA skill (category: compliance, tags: [HIPAA]) now matches
HIPAA workflow (category: compliance-posture) via category map +
workflow name keyword 'hipaa' matching skill tag 'HIPAA'.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: download VCLibs before Add-AppxPackage (PS5.1 has no -Uri param)

Add-AppxPackage -Uri is PS7+ only. PS5.1 throws:
  'A parameter cannot be found that matches parameter name Uri'
Fix: download to TEMP first, install from local path (same pattern
as UI Xaml and winget packages already used).

commit.message:
fix: refresh app token before benchmark push (#27)

* fix(ci): refresh app token before benchmark push

The GitHub App token generated at checkout expires after 1 hour.
The full benchmark suite takes longer than that, so by the time the
push step runs the token is stale.

Move token generation to right before the push step so it gets a
fresh token, and set the remote URL explicitly with it.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(benchmarks): allow better-sqlite3 native build in pnpm

better-sqlite3 was missing from onlyBuiltDependencies in
pnpm-workspace.yaml, so pnpm silently skipped compiling its native
addon during install. This caused all Node.js benchmark apps using
the db scenario to be skipped with a 'Could not locate the bindings
file' error.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: auto-install winget via AppxPackage if missing on fresh machine

Instead of hard-stopping with a manual install URL, now installs the
required dependencies and winget msixbundle automatically:
  1. VCLibs x64 14.00 Desktop (aka.ms URL)
  2. Microsoft.UI.Xaml 2.8 x64 (GitHub release)
  3. Microsoft.DesktopAppInstaller msixbundle (GitHub latest)

Falls back to manual URL only if auto-install fails.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Allow all users to create and manage API keys (#215)

* Allow all users to create and manage API keys

Move API key management from admin-only to user settings. All authenticated users can now create, view, and revoke their own API keys. Backend routes moved from /admin/api-keys to /me/api-keys, UI moved from dedicated admin page to Settings. Admin checks removed since the service layer already scopes keys per creator.

* Extract API keys UI into ApiKeysSection component

Address review feedback: extract the API key management UI (table, create/revoke/display modals) from Settings.svelte into a dedicated ApiKeysSection component. Also suppress 401 error toasts during loadApiKeys to avoid flashing errors when unauthenticated users hit /settings before the redirect to /login fires.

* Update frontend/src/routes.ts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Patrick Veverka <veverkap@users.noreply.github.com>

* Rename admin_api_key.go to api_key_handler.go

The handler file is no longer admin-specific since routes moved to /me. Update the filename and docs reference to match.

* Remove invalid string redirect for /admin/api-keys route

The "/admin/api-keys": "/settings" entry used a string value instead of a
Svelte component, which broke svelte-spa-router entirely and caused all
pages to fail to render. Simply remove the route since the admin API keys
page no longer exists.

* Update screenshot script for API keys in Settings

Remove the admin/api-keys entry from the admin pages list since that page
no longer exists. Add screenshots of the API Keys section within the
Settings page instead.

* Update docs/architecture.md

feat: disable bulk import — message param now required, batch scan preserved but disabled

Bulk channel scanning (lookback, channel sweep) is intentionally disabled
now that the initial data import is complete. Single-message imports via
the 'message' parameter remain the only active path.

Changes:
- bot/src/index.ts: message param is now required=true; lookback and
  channel options commented out with re-enable instructions
- importHandler/index.ts: removed batch scan dispatch; cooldown still
  enforced; all disabled code preserved in comments
- importHandler/validation.ts: simplified validateOptions() signature;
  parseLookback() and resolveScanChannel() preserved as comments

To re-enable batch scanning: uncomment the disabled blocks in all three
files and set message back to required=false.

commit.message:
Add PR comment notifications with deduplication

Created a new notification channel that posts comments directly on PRs
about conflicts, with built-in duplicate detection.

Features:
- ENABLE_PR_COMMENTS env var to opt-in (default: false)
- Posts comment on both PRs in a conflict pair
- Checks for existing comments before posting (signature + PR number)
- Integrates with deduplication (only comments on new/changed conflicts)
- Respects DRY_RUN mode (logs what would be posted)
- Comment includes: conflicting files, line ranges, other PR details

Comment format:
- Clear warning header with bot signature
- Link to conflicting PR with title
- List of files with overlapping line ranges
- Actionable guidance for developers
- Attribution to pr-conflict-detector

Duplicate prevention:
- Before posting, checks if comment already exists for this conflict pair
- If signature + other PR number found in existing comments, skips posting
- Error-safe: if check fails, assumes no comment to avoid blocking

Test coverage: 190 tests passing, 99% overall coverage, 9.92/10 pylint

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.description: A GitHub Action to detect potential merge conflicts between open pull requests

commit.message:
Fix RPC starvation: move block processing to spawn_blocking threads

Root cause: synchronous sled I/O inside add_block() (save, read-back
verify, scan_forward, undo_log, height update) runs directly on tokio
worker threads. During batch sync, 400+ sled operations per 50-block
batch saturate all 4 worker threads, starving the RPC accept loop,
timers, and networking — causing time-cli and dashboard to hang.

Changes:
- handle_blocks_response: wrap add_block_with_fork_handling in
  spawn_blocking + Handle::block_on so sled I/O runs on the blocking
  thread pool instead of worker threads
- server.rs: same spawn_blocking treatment for BlockResponse and
  BlockAnnouncement handlers
- add_block: skip post-save read-back hash verification during sync
  (halves sled I/O per block; verification still runs for non-sync blocks)
- add_block: skip scan_forward loop during sync (blocks arrive
  sequentially, no gaps to fill)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
